text: An operating loop is a durable thread with shared memory, tools that can act on the computer, ways to steer and resume the task, and a surface where the user can review the artifact itself.

evidence.quote: learning to give work an operating loop: a durable thread, shared memory, tools that can act on my computer, ways to steer and resume the task, and a surface where I can review the artifact itself.

text: Codex gives work somewhere to live, shifting from excellent coding tool to a broader productivity surface.

evidence.quote: Codex is still excellent for coding, but the more interesting shift is that it gives my work somewhere to live.

text: They accumulate history, preferences, and old decisions that I do not want to recreate every time I come back.

evidence.quote: They accumulate history, preferences, and old decisions that I do not want to recreate every time I come back.

issues.detail: 1 argument(s) not found in the quote/span — advisory only

text: Long-running threads trade off continuity against higher cost when revisited later because the conversation may no longer be in cache.

evidence.quote: Long-running threads are not free. If you revisit them later, the conversation is probably no longer in cache, so you can incur more cost than you would in a fresh short thread.

text: The vault is an Obsidian-based shared memory system that holds rolling context around work: people, decisions, open loops, daily notes, project state, and understanding that would otherwise get lost between threads.

evidence.quote: The vault holds rolling context around my work: people, decisions, open loops, daily notes, project state, and the bits of understanding that would otherwise get lost between threads.

text: Files force the agent to compress experience into a form that survives the thread, so if the thread dies, compacts badly, or becomes too expensive, the useful knowledge is preserved.

evidence.quote: Files force the agent to compress experience into a form that can survive the thread.
